_schema.description: Contract tested by tests/adversarial/test_adversarial_suite.py. For each fixture, declare the codes the validator MUST surface and the codes it MUST NOT surface. A future-only code (a detector that's on the roadmap but not yet implemented) lives under 'future_fire': those entries assert that if a matching check method is installed on the validator, it must catch the fixture — otherwise the case is skipped. Doc reference: docs/benchmark-hardening-analysis.md section 5.

fields.must_fire: codes required to appear in validation.issues (any severity satisfies)
fields.must_not_fire: codes that must NOT appear in validation.issues
fields.must_be_ok: when true, validator.report.ok must be True (no errors); when false, report.ok must be False; when null, the ok-state is not asserted (use for fixtures whose detectors are still WARNING-tier pending dependency beads)
fields.future_fire: codes gated on a detector that doesn't yet ship; suite skips the check when the detector symbol isn't importable
fields.extra_assertions: optional named bespoke checks run by the harness

pure_copy.description: model == human on every question — pure answer-key copy

public_copy_fake_private.description: public rows copy human; private rows fabricated from marginal

constant_offset.description: model[opt] = human[opt] + c, renormalized — constant-offset attack family

lied_aggregate.description: per-question honest; aggregate inflated to favourable values

zero_private_rows.description: submission omits the entire private 20% holdout

wrong_keys.description: fabricated rows use non-dataset keys + mismatched reported hash

null_agent.description: uniform distributions on every question — a legitimate baseline, NOT a fabrication

raw_response_desync.description: raw_text/selected_option disagrees with model_distribution top option
